item.title: The Nightcliff by-election is this weekend. Here is why it's happening and how to vote
item.description: Residents living in Darwin's northern suburbs of Nightcliff, Rapid Creek and parts of Coconut Grove will be heading to the polls this Saturday to vote in a by-election. It comes after the former member for Nightcliff resigned in February.

item.title: Man charged with manslaughter over death of Jarrad Antonovich in ayahuasca and kambo ceremony
item.description: Jarrad Antonovich died from a perforated oesophagus after allegedly participating in a ceremony involving South American hallucinogen ayahuasca and the frog poison, kambo.

item.title: Nightcliff by-election: Greens bid to hold Darwin seat challenged by three contenders
item.description: It started as a historic win for the Greens, but now the minor party is in for a fight to preserve its solitary seat in the Northern Territory Parliament.

item.title: One Nation selects agricultural businessman David Farley to contest Farrer by-election in NSW
item.description: David Farley, 69, defeats two other candidates in today's preselection battle at the Albury Entertainment Centre.

item.title: Labor on track to win Nightcliff by-election, taking NT's single Greens seat
item.description: Labor is on track to win the Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' first and only seat held in the Northern Territory parliament.

item.title: Bird deaths linked to fish lost in algal bloom, SA Research and Development Institute says
item.description: Birds are starving and dying along metropolitan Adelaide beaches, caused by a lack of fish as a result of the algal bloom, the South Australian government says.

item.title: After 40 years of parenthood incentives Singapore's fertility rate is still falling
item.description: Singapore is well-known for its social engineering to manage population growth. So why has the island city recorded its lowest-ever fertility rate again?

item.title: Shane Wanganeen sentenced for neglect of 7yo son Makai
item.description: A father who "prolonged his child's suffering" by failing to get him medical attention for 16 hours before his death, has been sentenced to 19 months in prison.

item.title: Fresh twist in case of child molester and former fugitive Charles Batham
item.description: There been a fresh twist in the long-running saga of child molester Charles Batham, with NSW authorities requesting his transfer from jail interstate to face new charges.

item.title: The Bikers Against Child Abuse helping kids reclaim a sense of safety
item.description: BACA members accompany kids to court, stand guard outside their homes, and provide a sense of safety with burly presences and deeply rumbling motorbikes.

item.title: These musicians stole our hearts on the first season of The Piano, so what are they up to now?
item.description: In the first season of The Piano, nobody quite knew what to expect. But what happens after you get the chance to play for a national audience? We catch up with some of the pianists to find out how music continues to shape their lives.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Return of the mums, retired shooter winds back the clock and Kiwis make hot start at Firebirds
item.description: The world's best goal keeper has sparked an important conversation about postpartum depression in her return to the Super Netball league.

item.title: Queensland's construction worker shortfall to peak at 35,000 ahead of 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games, report warns
item.description: With new figures laying bare the state of the problem, the construction industry warns cost blow-outs and delays are inevitable unless something is done.

item.title: Petrol price spiral could put mobile early childcare education services at risk
item.description: Mobile services travelling across regional New South Wales say rising petrol prices are putting early childcare education at risk.

item.title: Who is Anna Lapwood, the content creator bringing organ music to millions on social media
item.description: Anna Lapwood uses social media to share her love of the organ and favourite music from Bach to Lord of the Rings. Millions follow her and thousands more come to her concerts.

item.title: How award-winning pianist Andrea Lam learned to let go of perfection
item.description: She made her debut with Sydney Symphony Orchestra at 13, but don't call Andrea Lam a piano prodigy. The award-winning pianist shares how she's kept kicking career goals without losing her love of the music.

item.title: Home building industry facing COVID-like price spikes due to Middle East war
item.description: From sand to concrete, suppliers are bringing in "emergency fuel levies" as petrol prices soar due to the war in the Middle East. Major companies that supply pipes for plumbing are even warning of shortages.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Mannix starts Mums WhatsApp group, Wallam's revenge and Housby back to her best
item.description: Emily Mannix talks about her extended absence from the Super Netball league, and why she's now ready to return for the Melbourne Vixens after a "freak accident" meant her daughter Mabel needed emergency care at birth.

item.title: Critically endangered hawksbill sea turtle returns to wild months after beach rescue
item.description: What passers-by thought was a "sandy rock" was in fact a critically endangered turtle far from home.

item.title: How an Irukandji jellyfish the size of a fingernail causes 'unimaginable pain'
item.description: A tiny jellyfish found in Queensland has venom so powerful it causes feelings of "impending doom".

item.title: In a quest for an affordable mortgage my family tried apartment living
item.description: In a quest for an affordable mortgage in our location of choice, my family decide to rent an apartment as a trial. Will high-rise living be a realistic alternative to a house as a place to bring up our child?

item.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys WA banana crops, Carnarvon growers hit hard
item.description: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

item.title: Festival encourages people to see seaweed's 'importance and beauty'
item.description: South Australia's rugged south-east coast is known for its cold water smashing into limestone cliffs. But beneath the surface is a global hotspot of seaweed biodiversity.

item.title: Australia takes 2-0 lead in three-game ODI series against West Indies at Warner Park
item.description: Australia takes an unassailable 2-0 lead over the West Indies with a cruisy 91-run victory in the second ODI at St Kitts' Warner Park.

item.title: More than 200 buildings damaged in Exmouth, residents stranded as airport, roads remain closed
item.description: Visitors in Exmouth who were stuck there when Cyclone Narelle passed are now trying to find ways out of the town, but that is proving impossible for one grieving family.

item.title: Drivers winched to safety after ignoring road closures in WA's flooded Gascoyne following Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Authorities say motorists are repeatedly ignoring warnings not to drive in floodwaters in WA's north in the wake of Cyclone Narelle, with 20 people having to be rescued.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Kiera Austin creates period shorts, Kate Moloney collision raises safety questions and Mary Cholhok must lift
item.description: World Cup-winning Diamond Kiera Austin remembers having to learn to use a tampon earlier than she was ready in order to play netball. Now she's developed a period undershort she hopes will address the alarming rates of Australian teens dropping out of sport.

item.title: Tributes for farming stalwart Ian Burnett and grandson after farming accident
item.description: Tributes are flowing in for a central Queensland producer who died with his grandson when machinery rolled on a property at the weekend.

item.title: From screen to symphony: Joff Bush on Bluey’s new orchestral album and music from the hit series
item.description: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

item.title: NT tourism operators facing hundreds of thousands in damage costs after multiple devastating weather events
item.description: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

item.title: Sam Konstas left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for 2026-27 season
item.description: Sam Konstas is one of six players left off Cricket Australia's full contracts list for the upcoming season.

item.title: Algal bloom tracks further north up the Yorke Peninsula, sparking concern for stingrays
item.description: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

item.title: Interest in novated leases for electric vehicles soaring in Australia amid fuel crisis
item.description: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering getting an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

item.title: Alleged cocaine smugglers' three-month journey from Panama to Sydney ends in arrest
item.description: Setting off from Panama just before Christmas, police say the MV Raider started its journey towards Sydney with almost 6 tonnes of cocaine aboard worth nearly $2 billion.

item.title: Tim David stars in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's win over Chennai Super Kings
item.description: It is a mixed day for the Australians in the Indian Premier League, with Tim David powering RCB to a victory, while Travis Head fails to make an impact in Sunrisers Hyderabad's loss in Lucknow.

item.title: Man found dead after crossing floodwaters on foot in SA's far north
item.description: Police have found the body of a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Social media posts educating public about illicit drugs being removed by Meta, Australian health experts say
item.description: Public health workers are warning lives could be at risk due to social media sites automatically censoring educational posts about illicit drugs circulating in the community.

item.title: Childcare crisis hurts families facing two-year wait in Goondiwindi region
item.description: In the depths of grief, this family wants to grow. But a childcare crisis stands in its way.

item.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?
item.description: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

item.title: TV quiz: Match the quote to the classic Australian television show
item.description: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

item.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over death of Friends star Matthew Perry
item.description: Jayvee Sangha admits to running a "stash house" that helped supply the famous Friends star ketamine in the months before his death.

item.title: Severe Cyclone Maila moving again towards Far North Queensland after stalling in Solomon Sea
item.description: Cyclone Maila has woken up and is heading west, with the category four system still likely to reach Queensland's far north on Monday.

item.title: UK man who sexually abused girl has visa reinstated over 'strong ties' to Australia
item.description: The man's permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

item.title: Regular unleaded petrol to peak in May — as it happened
item.description: Westpac has drawn a line in the sand and said unleaded petrol prices are projected to peak at $2.46 a litre in late May, with a 26 cents increase when excise returns to normal. Look back at how the trading session unfolded with our specialist business reporters.

item.title: Do you ever need to throw out your old bath towels?
item.description: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Construction sector warns of housing delays, mass lay-offs due to Middle East war
item.description: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

item.title: ASX 200 and Aussie dollar down, Brent crude and WTI oil up — as it happened
item.description: The Australian market has finished the trading week on a disappointing note, dropping slightly, despite US stocks rising overnight. Look back on our business blog.

item.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
item.description: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

item.title: Rotting sperm whale carcass forces relocation of Tasmania's largest open water swim
item.description: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

item.title: Life as a human cannonball.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Liberals to offer $5,000 to overseas officers to relocate under plan to recruit 3,000 police
item.description: The opposition says officers from the UK, Ireland and New Zealand would be offered $5,000 to relocate under a plan to recruit 3,000 new officers.

item.title: Police locate hiker who sheltered overnight after missing for 24 hours in Victoria's north-west
item.description: Police have located a hiker who separated from his group and was missing for more than 24 hours in Victoria's north-west.

item.title: Coral Coast tourism hit with mass cancellations after Cyclone Narelle
item.description: A whale shark boat skipper says one-off payments for tour operators will make a "small difference", as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle's aftermath drags on.

item.title: How these queer couples divide the mental load in their household
item.description: After publishing our story on the four stages of the mental load, we asked to hear from queer couples about how they divide and conquer the cognitive and emotional labour at home.

item.title: Business confidence crashes to lowest level since April 2020 — as it happened
item.description: The outbreak of the war in the Middle East saw business confidence post its second-largest monthly fall on record, a NAB survey found.

item.title: Police thank Nannup community after 87-year-old bushwalker rescued
item.description: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

item.title: Super Netball round-up: Expansion pushed back and the growing trend to call greats out of retirement
item.description: Two new teams were expected to join the league in 2027 to make Super Netball a 10-team competition, but slow broadcast negotiations are holding up the process.

item.title: States to reveal if they support privatisation of Big Bash League clubs, with possible IPL part-ownership
item.description: Cricket Australia will know in the coming days if the states are happy to privatise their Big Bash League clubs, a call that may see them become part-owned by Indian Premier League franchises.

item.title: Australia's Cameron Green' poor IPL season continues in Kolkata Knight Riders' loss to Chennai Super Kings
item.description: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

item.title: Farmers selling cattle in record numbers as feed becomes scarce and El Niño forecast
item.description: More than 500,000 head of cattle have already been sold across New South Wales this year, with Scone seeing its biggest sale in 20 years.

item.title: Unemployment remains at 4.3pc, ASX closes lower — as it happened
item.description: The ASX closes lower after the release of ABS labour force data showing the unemployment remains at 4.3 per cent in seasonally adjusted terms. Viva Energy is in a trading halt after a fire at its Geelong oil refinery.

item.title: LIV Golf CEO says 2026 season will not be interrupted despite rumours Saudi Arabia PIF may cut funding
item.description: LIV Golf will run as planned in 2026 according to the league's CEO, as media reports suggest its funding from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund may soon end.

item.title: Bank warns of significant surge in inflation as oil price spikes — as it happened
item.description: The Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) expects Australian inflation to surge to 6 per cent, while adding another RBA interest rate hike to its forecast.

item.title: French Open bans cameras in player areas after Melbourne criticism
item.description: Organisers of the French Open have promised that their players will enjoy camera-free privacy in a space of their own, following criticism the Australian Open copped from top stars this year.

item.title: Before the timer reaches zero, 100 bombs have dropped
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces labelled it Operation Eternal Darkness. Inside Lebanon, it's now called Black Wednesday. ABC NEWS Verify has geolocated the explosions across 10 minutes of chaos.

item.title: Mental health nurse Adam McGovern jailed for sexually abusing 14-year-old patient
item.description: A former West Australian mental health nurse is sentenced to 12 years in jail after being convicted for persistent sexual abuse of a teenager and making "upskirting" videos of women and children.

item.title: Aung San Suu Kyi to be freed earlier as Myanmar releases ex-president Win Myint from jail
item.description: Myanmar's ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi has her prison sentence cut as thousands of prisoners are freed. Meanwhile, the UN reveals 2025 marked the deadliest year on record for Rohingya refugees fleeing Myanmar.

item.title: The men tricked into helping drug smugglers import Fiji's largest meth haul
item.description: Two men have told Foreign Correspondent how they were unwittingly drawn into Fiji's largest-ever meth importation.

item.title: The Australians keeping a century of rainfall records as stations disappear
item.description: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

item.title: Pale octopus species mysteriously disappears from SA's Spencer Gulf
item.description: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 20: Can you tame our tricky questions?
item.description: Is two-up actually illegal in Australia? And what movie beat Crocodile Dundee to the top of the box office in 1986?

item.title: Australian golfer Hannah Green wins fourth title in five starts with play-off victory in LA Championship
item.description: Australian golf ace Hannah Green wins her fourth title of the season, taking out the LPGA Tour's LA Championship for a third time in four years.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: How many games will be shown on free-to-air TV in the Nine broadcast deal?
item.description: Uncertainties over the details of Netball Australia's broadcast agreement with Nine left fans hesitant to celebrate. Now we reveal how many games will actually be seen on free-to-air TV.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Second death of Aboriginal inmate in Bandyup Women's Prison sparks call for review
item.description: Calls are mounting for a "transparent" government review of WA's Bandyup Women's Prison after a second Aboriginal inmate dies inside the facility in less than five weeks.

item.title: Vanuatu denies it is about to sign a security deal with China as Australia works to ink its own pact
item.description: As Australia presses on to finally sign a key agreement with Vanuatu, reports of a security agreement with China have drawn a fierce response from Port Vila.

item.title: NSW council investigates Kempsey Shire councillor over online comments
item.description: A Kempsey Shire councillor is being investigated for misconduct after allegedly claiming a woman should have been "bashed" online.

item.title: Support services nationwide 'buckling under demand' during Sexual Assault Awareness Month
item.description: As Australia marks Sexual Assault Awareness Month, experts and victim-survivors say sexual violence remains pervasive and under-reported.

item.title: Remote NT residents evacuated to Batchelor say security 'treating us like prisoners'
item.description: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

item.title: Stump decorated as its remains left on Adelaide street for seven months
item.description: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

item.title: Leading Trump envoy asks FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup amid Pope Leo XIV attacks
item.description: As the US president feuds with Pope Leo, one of his top envoys suggests giving Italy a World Cup spot could mend fences.

item.title: Preet Singh to return as Wyndham City Council mayor after character reference scandal
item.description: Embattled mayor Preet Singh says he will return to the role after stepping aside earlier this month amid revelations that he provided a character reference to a convicted child sex offender.

item.title: Garry Charles Kingston, husband of former WA Nationals MLC Louise Kingston, hit with child sex abuse charges
item.description: Former Nationals and Independent MLC Louise Kingston watched on as her husband Garry faced fresh child sexual abuse charges in a WA court.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O counter-sued by radio show broadcaster
item.description: The company that broadcast the Kyle and Jackie O Show has launched a counter claim against the duo seeking damages for breach of contract, losses in profit and advertising revenue, and legal costs.

item.title: Matildas goalkeeper Chloe Lincoln on finding her voice and commanding her idols
item.description: The A-League Women finals kick off this weekend, with Brisbane to make its first appearance in five years.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot
item.description: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

item.title: Is it ever OK to use someone else's toothbrush?
item.description: About half of us have shared a toothbrush — usually between spouses, or parents and children. We asked the experts if it's a good idea, even in an emergency.

item.title: Resilient Queensland bush community keeps Eidsvold Cattle Drive alive
item.description: A dedicated band of volunteers is keeping a cattle drive tradition alive to raise money for vital health services.

item.title: Horse trainer wants Australia to remember light horsemen, their 'amazing' companions this Anzac Day
item.description: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

item.title: Kingborough mayor Paula Wriedt to resign, citing toxic behaviour from councillors
item.description: A greater Hobart mayor will resign after serving in a public role for three decades, saying 'keyboard warriors' and a 'toxic culture' from councillors prompted her decision.

item.title: Gas giant Woodside says additional tax on gas exports would kill projects at Senate inquiry
item.description: Oil and gas giant Woodside denies its business model is "weak" while arguing that paying a proposed flat tax on gas exports would kill the company's projects.

item.title: Berri Barmera councillor convicted of drink driving able to keep licence until after Anzac Day
item.description: Berri Barmera Councillor Ian Schlein will hand in his licence several days after a regional court convicted him of drink driving, to allow him to attend an Anzac Day parade.

item.title: Is Something For Kate frontman Paul Dempsey Australia's best cover artist?
item.description: From Taylor Swift to Cher, Paul Dempsey's cover versions have been lauded. But does he have a secret weapon to making the perfect cover?

item.title: With Mojtaba Khamenei a 'symbolic sovereign', who is running Iran?
item.description: After a wave of assassinations targeting top Iranian officials and a supreme leader who has not been seen in public since the war began, a key question has emerged: who is really running Iran?

item.title: The outcome of the Farrer by-election could be a watershed moment in Australian politics
item.description: The Coalition's preference deals in Farrer could help fracture the Liberal Party's moderate base and invigorate dissatisfied voters.

item.title: Farmers in WA food bowl region take $25 million cyclone hit
item.description: Growers across Western Australia's Gascoyne take a huge financial hit after four natural disasters in the first four months of 2026.

item.title: Ringo Starr brought country music to The Beatles — now he's getting back to it
item.description: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

item.title: Booing at Anzac Day services shows lack of public awareness of racism in Australia
item.description: Jeers could be heard for more than a minute as Uncle Ray Minniecon spoke at yesterday's Anzac service in Sydney with similar scenes playing out in Melbourne and Perth.

item.title: Australia's lucrative drug market is fuelling a wave of narco subs crossing the Pacific
item.description: At least seven narco subs have been discovered in Pacific waters in recent years, signalling a troubling new reality.

item.title: NT police have 'mounting concerns' for missing five-year-old girl Sharon in Alice Springs
item.description: Northern Territory police believe five-year-old Sharon may have walked out the front door of her home in the middle of the night.

item.title: Why street artist Peter Drew decided to bring back his 'Aussie' poster series
item.description: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

item.title: Canberra is growing its economic 'pie' to become more than a city of public administration
item.description: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

item.title: Frustration with war effort sparks renewed push to bring down Netanyahu — as it happened
item.description: Former Israeli prime ministers Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid are merging their two parties into a new entity called "Together" in a bid to defeat Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the next election.

item.title: The history of the Moonie Oil Fields in Queensland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sheep farmers swear by Lifetime Ewe course to steer them through drought and diesel price spikes
item.description: With a quick feel of a sheep's back, farmers score their flock, finding the magic numbers to help them navigate record-breaking drought, and now diesel price spikes.

item.title: Four easy egg recipes for any occasion
item.description: If you're looking to add the humble egg into your weekly meals, we have you covered.

item.title: Prediction markets accused of insider trading and profiting from state secrets
item.description: A US soldier accused of using classified information to place bets on a covert military operation has thrown a spotlight on the fast-growing world of prediction markets.

item.title: RSL Australia to review Welcome to Country policies after elders booed on Anzac Day
item.description: RSL Australia will review its Welcome to Country guidelines after Indigenous elders were booed during Anzac Day services across the country.

item.title: CFMEU administrator Mark Irving KC resigns
item.description: Mark Irving KC says the next "rebuild phase" of the union requires someone with "a different set of skills".

item.title: The best ways to protect credit and debit cards from fraud
item.description: Experts reveal the most common ways fraudsters steal card details and how to keep your accounts safer.

item.title: The security breach that exposed Donald Trump to a shooting incident
item.description: A shooting incident at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has prompted a swirl of questions about how an alleged shooter breached Secret Service security and got so close to the US president.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Farmer rescues kangaroo from mud in dry dam as drought conditions hit western NSW
item.description: Photos show a kangaroo moments before being rescued from a dam in Central West NSW, after it became bogged in mud while searching for water in an increasingly barren landscape.

item.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
item.description: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

item.title: Wyndham councillors pass vote of no confidence in mayor Preet Singh over character reference scandal
item.description: Wyndham councillors in Melbourne's south-west unanimously pass a vote of no confidence in Mayor Preet Singh over a character reference he provided to a convicted child sex offender.

item.title: CCTV to be installed on Gold Coast waterways to crack down on illegal boaties
item.description: The Gold Coast Waterways Authority says the initiative comes after more than 400 unregistered and unseaworthy vessels were removed from the city's waterways over the past five years.

item.title: ASX loses ground, Bank of Japan keeps rates at 0.75pc — as it happened
item.description: Australia's share market lost ground on Tuesday, and Brent crude oil futures rose back above $US100 a barrel, while the Bank of Japan kept its overnight rate at 0.75pc. Catch up on the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
item.description: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

item.title: Former Australian federal commissioner Tom Rogers to lead independent review into SA election
item.description: Former Australian electoral commissioner Tom Rogers is appointed to lead a "sweeping independent review" of the management and oversight of last month's SA state election and First Nations Voice election.

item.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
item.description: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Melbourne's Yarra River probably hunting prey, researcher says
item.description: Researchers say the dolphin appears healthy and is most likely chasing prey in the Melbourne waterway.

item.title: Single-use plastic soy sauce fish are an eco-nightmare. A local designer has a solution
item.description: Shoyu-tai, the single-use plastic soy sauce fish that accompany takeaway sushi, are an eco-nightmare — but a local designer has a solution.

item.title: Rockhampton's Plaza Hotel set to go under the hammer, but no inspections allowed
item.description: Rockhampton's Plaza Hotel is set to go under the hammer after closing down more than a decade ago.

item.title: Memorial service held to remember and reflect on Port Arthur massacre's enduring legacy, 30 years on
item.description: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

item.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago as historians search for murder scene
item.description: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.

item.title: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa spared jail over child abuse material
item.description: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa is spared jail after a court found her novel fetishised young children.

item.title: Penny Wong to press Asian countries for fuel guarantees during regional tour
item.description: As Australia grapples with energy supply issues, Penny Wong travels to Japan to stress the importance of an ongoing supply of fuel.

item.title: Saudi Arabia's Reality Check
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: UAE leaves OPEC in major blow to global oil producers' group
item.description: It comes as unprecedented energy crisis triggered by the Iran war exposes splits among Gulf nations.

item.title: King Charles says 'acts of violence will never succeed' after attack on Trump — as it happened
item.description: King Charles has delivered a speech to Congress as part of his visit to the US to celebrate the 250th anniversary of American independence.

item.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
item.description: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

item.title: Does the gas industry pay its fair share of tax?
item.description: Advocates are proposing a flat 25 per cent tax on gas exports, but representatives say the industry is being unfairly targeted.

item.title: Queenslanders urged to get tested for viral hepatitis after liver cancer deaths triple since 1980s
item.description: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

item.title: SEQ first-home buyers turn to Melbourne as 5% Deposit Scheme provides limited opportunity
item.description: South-East Queensland has been under-represented in 5% Deposit Scheme approvals, with prospective first-home buyers heading to Melbourne for its "considerable affordability" benefits.

item.title: Parents slam 'weak' response at The Friends' School after girls targeted in AI deepfake pornography scandal
item.description: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

item.title: Western NSW dam limit increase plan dumped over impact on protected wetlands
item.description: Water users in part of the Murray-Darling Basin are anxious that a proposal to protect their interests during droughts has been abandoned, as the dry conditions continue to worsen.

item.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
item.description: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

item.title: Can the SA government justify a $45m golf course upgrade without LIV Golf?
item.description: The Malinauskas government took a calculated risk to redevelop the North Adelaide Golf Course in time for LIV Golf in 2028. It might not pay off.

item.title: Inflation rises to highest level since 2023, ASX drops for seventh consecutive day — as it happened
item.description: ABS data shows a rise in inflation to 4.6 per cent in March, the highest since September 2023, after another negative day for the ASX with the Reserve Bank widely expected to lift interest rates again next week. Look back on our live blog.

item.title: Family of Queensland grandmother who died after weight-loss surgery says her death was preventable
item.description: Joanne Downie underwent an elective gastric bypass surgery so she could live a healthier life for her grandchildren, but she died six days later in a regional Queensland hospital.

item.title: LIV Golf postpones United States event amid tour's uncertain future
item.description: LIV Golf and the US state of Louisiana make a call on the tour's event in New Orleans, which was slated for June.

item.title: US regulator orders review of ABC licence after Donald Trump criticises Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: The search for missing girl Sharon Granites
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ultra-swimmer Andy Donaldson sets world record with 55km Ord River challenge
item.description: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

item.title: Robyn Lambley speaks on the search for Sharon Granites
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Brodie Dewar, accused killer of Labor identity Tim Picton, pleads not guilty to manslaughter
item.description: The man accused of killing former Labor identity Tim Picton outside a Northbridge venue has pleaded not guilty to his manslaughter.

item.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

item.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain
item.description: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

item.title: What is OPEC? Why is the UAE leaving it?
item.description: The UAE is leaving an international oil-producers' group that has a major influence on the price of oil around the world.

item.title: AFL confirms draft changes despite 'disingenuous' opposition from clubs
item.description: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

item.title: Former police officer loses appeal over conviction for assaulting teens during arrest in Batemans Bay
item.description: A former NSW police officer has failed in an attempt to overturn a conviction for using unreasonable force in the arrest of two teenage boys.

item.title: Man accused of hit-and-run murder of ex-partner at Daisy Hill committed to stand trial
item.description: Tyler Scott Willian James has been charged with the murder of his ex-partner Kiesha Thompson, 23, who was struck by a vehicle in July, 2024.

item.title: War hero and veteran MP Bill Grayden dies aged 105
item.description: WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, is being remembered for his service to his country and community.

item.title: Countries including Australia on the hunt for new sources of oil
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Missing girl Sharon Granites's family urges anyone with information to come forward
item.description: Family members of a missing Alice Springs five-year-old have spoken to the ABC for the first time, pleading for anyone with information that could help find their "energetic" little girl to come forward.

item.title: Interpreter shortages leaving non-English speakers at risk in court, advocates say
item.description: As Brisbane courts face delays and backlogs, cases involving people with limited English are being adjourned when interpreters are unavailable — a problem advocates warn is set to worsen.

item.title: Converting from gas to electricity could save consumers hundreds but changeover costs high
item.description: A new report says the money consumers could save by converting from gas to electricity is set to increase over the coming decades, sparking calls for government regulation to encourage the switch.

item.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
item.description: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

item.title: Bodycam footage shows police officers subduing London attacker
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Miranda Priestly makes a splashy return in The Devil Wears Prada 2
item.description: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

item.title: Counterterrorism intelligence's share of funding in 'significant' decline since 2020, royal commission finds
item.description: Royal commissioner Virginia Bell says classified documents reveal a significant shift in intelligence spending away from counterterrorism since 2020, in the first findings of what failings led to last year's Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Western Queensland's Pilungah Reserve third to gain special status ending mineral prospecting permits
item.description: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

item.title: Parents of two children killed in Bowen Mountain house fire thank first responders and community
item.description: A statement shared by the parents of two children killed in a Bowen Mountain house fire say their "family's world has been forever changed".

item.title: NSW Police under scrutiny over resources allocated to targeted Bondi Beach Hanukkah event
item.description: NSW Police officers assigned to patrol the Hanukkah celebration targeted in the Bondi Beach terrorist attack were told they did not need to "stay the entire duration" of the event, despite members of the Jewish community fearing it was at risk.

item.title: RACQ denies misleading customers with insurance notices amid ASIC Federal Court lawsuit
item.description: When the corporate regulator accused RACQ of misleading customers with insurance renewal notices, the insurer apologised. Now, it is defending the notices in court filings.

item.title: Victorian bottle shops awash with illicit alcohol, researchers find
item.description: The National Drug Research Institute found 30 per cent of bottle shops it audited across different areas of Melbourne and regional Victoria were stocking illicit alcohol.

item.title: Savers Australia denies allegations of bed bugs at Brunswick thrift store
item.description: Thrift store chain Savers Australia has denied there is evidence of bed bugs at their popular Brunswick shop, as allegations of an outbreak continue to circulate online.

item.title: Moore goals after siren as Hawks and Pies settle for thrilling draw
item.description: A goal after the siren from Dylan Moore allows Hawthorn to claim a draw against Collingwood at the MCG.

item.title: NRL coach Craig Bellamy diagnosed with form of neurodegenerative disorder
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy has been diagnosed with a form of neurodegenerative disorder, the club has said.

item.title: Jefferson Lewis arrested over alleged murder of Alice Springs girl Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Jefferson Lewis has been the subject of a police manhunt, and is accused of murdering the young girl in Alice Springs.

item.title: How two 'Origin plays' turned the tide for New South Wales against Queensland
item.description: With Queensland on the verge of another classic ambush, it took two "Origin plays" for New South Wales to turn the tide in the women's series opener in Newcastle.

item.title: Police use tear gas amid violent scenes outside Alice Springs Hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A Trump shooting scare, a stunt gone wrong, a burger blue and a rescued roo
item.description: It's time to sit back after a big week in news and test how much you remember.

item.title: Seasonal produce for all your autumn soups, roasts and salads
item.description: From carrots to cauliflower, here is some of the best seasonal produce to enjoy in your autumn soups, roasts and salads.

item.title: Bond yields at crisis level as Australian government's 10-Year Treasury rate passes 5 per cent
item.description: The 10-Year Treasury bond, which the Commonwealth issues to raise money, has pushed past 5 per cent, raising the cost of paying off debt and suggesting inflation is not under control.

item.title: The home battery rebate is changing. Is it still worth it and what's the right size?
item.description: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

item.title: The Bondi royal commission interim report is careful but ultimately damning
item.description: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

item.title: Live: Iran threatens 'long and painful strikes' if US renews attacks
item.description: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei says Tehran will eliminate "the enemies' abuses of the waterway" under new management of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

item.title: New photo of Aung San Suu Kyi released as military says she is moved from prison to house arrest
item.description: Myanmar's detained former president Aung San Suu Kyi has been moved from prison to house arrest, according to state media.

item.title: National average house prices rose slightly in April, the slowest pace of growth since January 2025
item.description: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

item.title: China using a double-insurance strategy to secure crude oil supplies amid Iran war
item.description: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Violence erupts as rioters clash with police outside Alice Springs Hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pacific nations aim to tackle regional unrest with expanded Pacific Response Group
item.description: The Pacific Response Group is beginning negotiations on a treaty that would allow it to respond more quickly to natural disasters and other crises across the region.

item.title: The Fitzroy Community School has been the subject of complaints over the years.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Philip O'Carroll speaks in an FCS home video.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Clancy walking in Melbourne
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'No secrets': Alternative school's history of discipline in spotlight
item.description: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

item.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says
item.description: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

item.title: Former Canberra surgeon to face disciplinary action for perjury, attempting to pervert the course of justice
item.description: Former Canberra vascular surgeon Imran Kader will face disciplinary action after he lied under oath and tried to interfere with a witness during criminal proceedings for an alleged rape.

item.title: Mount Isa City Council moves to sell homes over unpaid rates in outback mining city
item.description: In Mount Isa, nearly 50 properties, some abandoned, have accrued years of unpaid rates and could be forced to sale.

item.title: Canadian authorities search for missing Australian hiker Denise Ann Williams in Nova Scotia
item.description: Canadian police are searching for a Victorian woman who disappeared while hiking on an island on the country's east coast.

item.title: Live: Wong confirms discussions with US over proposal to open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Penny Wong has confirmed Australia is in discussions with the US over its latest proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

item.title: Saudi Arabia confirms it will no longer fund LIV Golf as future of league hangs in the balance
item.description: Australia's Cameron Smith is one of several big names in golf left with an uncertain future after Saudi Arabia pulled funding of LIV Golf.

item.title: Extreme heat the most deadly yet preventable natural disaster, NSW Net Zero Commission says
item.description: The agency set up by the Minns government just two years ago says extreme heat is the most deadly yet most preventable natural hazard facing Australians.

item.title: Victoria Police doubles recruit intake to 1,300 a year as it tries to fill hundreds of vacancies
item.description: The police force is trying to fill a shortfall in frontline officers with crime in Victoria at a 10-year high.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood smashed for 24 off one over as Shubman Gill fires in the IPL
item.description: Shubman Gill takes Australian quick Josh Hazlewood to task as Gujarat took down Bengaluru in the IPL.

item.title: Ashley Youth Detention Centre cameras offline during lockdown incident
item.description: Security cameras at Ashley Youth Detention Centre were offline when a detainee threatened a staff member with a knife during a cooking workshop, documents reveal.

item.title: Left in the red dirt: The forgotten trail of Australia's longest strike
item.description: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.
